100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On March 20, 1926, a significant political shift unfolded as the British Parliament debated the future of Irish independence, with tensions rising over the Government of Ireland Bill. Meanwhile, in the realm of aviation, American aviator Charles Lindbergh announced plans for a transatlantic flight, igniting widespread public excitement. In the United States, Prohibition continued to spur debates over law enforcement and bootlegging, as the nation grappled with the social impacts of alcohol bans.
Updated: Friday, March 20, 2026 at 07:27:19 AM CDT
